SelectionRationale: A recognized finite benchmark that is neither adversarial nor custom-built for the represented predicates; large enough to require meaningful state-space search but small enough for independent exact replay.
BeforeState: The canonical demo had exact finite fixtures and calibration evidence, but no independently replayable exact-search comparison on a named TSPLIB instance after the glossary freeze.
PlannedClosureCriterion: Using the unmodified GR17 matrix, obtain a feasible tour of published value 2085 without consulting the optimum during construction; independently prove that no cheaper tour exists; compare exact state-space effort with Held-Karp dynamic programming; coin no new active term.

Method.ProofSearch: best-first Search-State Quotient over (last city, unvisited set)
Method.CompletionEnvelope: minimum edge from the current endpoint into the unvisited set + MST(unvisited) + minimum edge from the unvisited set to the depot
Method.Dominance: retain only the cheapest prefix for each (last city, unvisited set)
Method.Pruning: discard a state when prefix cost plus Completion Envelope is at least the incumbent tour cost

AfterState: The frozen vocabulary expresses a reproducible exact solution of GR17: a standard constructive portfolio supplies value 2085, and the MST completion envelope exhausts every potentially cheaper Search-State Quotient state. The proof expands 4.07% as many states as full Held-Karp dynamic programming on this instance.
Nonclaims: This does not compete with Concorde on wall-clock performance.
This does not establish a generally superior exact TSP algorithm.
The comparison is against Held-Karp state enumeration, not against industrial branch-and-cut.
One benchmark is evidence of executable coherence, not broad empirical superiority.
